name: Ecuador (Republic of Ecuador / República del Ecuador)
summary: Ecuador is an Andean South American civil-law republic whose family-law framework operates under the Civil Code (Código Civil), supplemented by the Children and Adolescents Code 2003 (Código de la Niñez y Adolescencia, Law 100). Parental authority (patria potestad) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 283-307 and Children and Adolescents Code Title V. The National Court of Justice (Corte Nacional de Justicia) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Corte Constitucional) operates separate constitutional review. The 2008 Constitution recognises indigenous jurisdictional pluralism under art. 171. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Family, Women, Children and Adolescents Courts (Unidades Judiciales de Familia, Mujer, Niñez y Adolescencia). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministerio de Salud Pública licensing framework with the Federación Ecuatoriana de Psicólogos operating professional standards. Ecuador is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the superior-interest-of-the-child standard codified in Children and Adolescents Code art. 11. Ecuador acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 April 1992 — earliest Latin American accession in the corpus.

statutory_framework.title: Civil Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code based on Bello Civil Code (Chilean origin) with subsequent modernisation. Arts. 283-307 govern patria potestad and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Children and Adolescents Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children and Adolescents Code codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle aligned with UNCRC obligations.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution — Indigenous Jurisdiction
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitutional recognition of indigenous jurisdictional pluralism.

apex_courts.name: National Court of Justice (Corte Nacional de Justicia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Corte Constitucional)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministerio de Salud Pública, Ecuador
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

professional_regulators.name: Federación Ecuatoriana de Psicólogos
professional_regulators.role: Peak professional federation for psychologists in Ecuador.

anonymisation_convention: Ecuadorian family-court decisions are anonymised per National Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Battle of Pichincha + Ecuador independence + Gran-Colombia framework
key_developments.description: Battle of Pichincha 24 May 1822 — Ecuador independence from Spain under José de Sucre and Simón Bolívar. Subsequent Gran-Colombia framework 1822-1830 with Venezuela and Colombia. Foundational pre-modern-codification framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Civil Code (Bello-influenced)
key_developments.description: Federal Civil Code enacted 1860 based on Bello Civil Code (Chilean origin) with subsequent modernisation. Arts. 283-307 govern patria potestad and child custody. Foundational substantive-civil-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Ecuador ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child + 1990s-economic-crisis framework
key_developments.description: Ecuador ratified the UNCRC on 23 March 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2003 Children and Adolescents Code substantive doctrine. 1999 financial-crisis substantively significant Latin-American economic-instability framework.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession
key_developments.description: Ecuador acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 April 1992 — earliest Latin American accession in the corpus. Substantively significant Andean-South-American cross-border-jurisdiction framework.

key_developments.title: Dollarisation 2000 + 21st-century-Latin-American-distinctive framework
key_developments.description: Ecuador adopted US Dollar 9 January 2000 ending Sucre currency framework — substantively distinctive Latin American dollarisation framework (alongside El Salvador 2001). Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory affecting subsequent cross-border-jurisdiction-practice.

key_developments.title: Children and Adolescents Code (Law 100)
key_developments.description: Federal Code enacted Law 100 of 3 January 2003 codifying superior-interest-of-the-child principle (art. 11) aligned with UNCRC obligations. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within Andean South American framework.

key_developments.title: Rafael Correa + Citizen Revolution + 21st-century-socialism framework
key_developments.description: Rafael Correa (PAIS Alliance) inaugurated President 15 January 2007 — substantively distinctive Latin American 21st-century-socialism framework. Subsequent Constitution 2008 + extended-presidency 2007-2017 substantively significant constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution + Indigenous Jurisdiction recognition + Plurinational-state framework
key_developments.description: Constitution adopted 28 September 2008 by referendum recognising indigenous jurisdictional pluralism (art. 171) — substantively distinctive Latin American Plurinational-state framework (with Bolivia). Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence including indigenous-jurisdiction framework.

key_developments.title: Corte Constitucional same-sex marriage decision + Sentencia 11-18-CN/19 framework
key_developments.description: Corte Constitucional de Ecuador Sentencia 11-18-CN/19 of 12 June 2019 declaring constitutionality of same-sex marriage following Inter-American Court of Human Rights Advisory Opinion OC-24/17 (24 November 2017) — substantively distinctive globally only Andean-Constitutional-Court-driven same-sex marriage framework + structurally distinctive Latin American constitutional-court-implementing-OC-24/17 framework + substantive Civil Code amendments for same-sex marriage + adoption-by-same-sex-couples framework + foundational substantive same-sex-family framework affecting subsequent superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Children and Adolescents Code (Law 100) framework + Corte Nacional de Justicia substantive register evolution.

key_developments.title: Ecuador security-crisis + Noboa-presidency + cartel-violence framework
key_developments.description: Ecuador security crisis 2018-onwards substantively distinctive Latin American post-Pacific-dispatch-route security framework — drug-cartel-violence 8 January 2024 substantively affecting demographic and family-law-implementation framework. Daniel Noboa inaugurated President 23 November 2023. Substantive constitutional-democratic-trajectory affecting subsequent family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Código Orgánico de la Función Judicial reform + Consejo de la Judicatura + Defensoría del Pueblo framework
key_developments.description: Código Orgánico de la Función Judicial reform 2020-2022 strengthening judicial framework. Consejo de la Judicatura and Defensoría del Pueblo institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Children and Adolescents Code 2003 + Civil Code framework. Multiple Corte Nacional de Justicia decisions developing superior-interest-of-the-child interpretation including post-2020-pandemic adjustment framework. Substantively significant Andean institutional jurisprudential development establishing trajectory toward 2023 security-crisis + Noboa presidency context.

key_developments.title: Corte Nacional de Justicia + Corte Constitucional — superior-interest-of-the-child substantive register + Noboa presidency
key_developments.description: Corte Nacional de Justicia and Corte Constitucional continue to develop superior-interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Civil Code arts. 283-307 + Children and Adolescents Code 2003 art. 11 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Noboa presidency framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Ecuador operates a Spanish-civil-law family-law framework drawing on Bello Civil Code (Chilean origin) heritage — places Ecuador in the Andean South American civil-law cluster with Peru, Bolivia, Colombia + Bello-Civil-Code-derivative sub-cluster.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 1992 places Ecuador as the earliest Latin American Hague accession within the corpus.
Constitution 2008 indigenous jurisdiction recognition aligns Ecuador with Bolivia within the indigenous-jurisdictional-pluralism cluster.
editorial_notes: Ecuador jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Battle-of-Pichincha-to-Noboa trajectory: 1822-Battle-of-Pichincha-+-Ecuador-independence-+-Gran-Colombia + 1860-Civil-Code-Bello-influenced + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1992-Hague-Convention-1980-accession-earliest-Latin-American + 2000-Dollarisation + 2003-Children-and-Adolescents-Code + 2007-Rafael-Correa-+-Citizen-Revolution-+-21st-century-socialism + 2008-Constitution-+-Indigenous-Jurisdiction-+-Plurinational-state + 2023-Ecuador-security-crisis-+-Noboa-+-cartel-violence + 2024-Corte-Nacional-+-Corte-Constitucional.

Civil-law Andean South America (Civil Code Bello-derivative + Children and Adolescents Code 2003 + Constitution 2008 indigenous jurisdiction + Hague Convention 1980 accession 1992 — earliest Latin American).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive superior-interest-of-the-child analysis under Civil Code arts. 283-307 + Children and Adolescents Code 2003 art. 11 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Andean + civil-law + Bello-Civil-Code-derivative (with Chile, Colombia) + indigenous-jurisdiction-constitutional-recognition-art-171-Plurinational-state (with Bolivia) + earliest-Latin-American-Hague-Convention-1992 + Dollarisation-2000-Latin-American-distinctive (with El Salvador 2001) + Rafael-Correa-21st-century-socialism + Battle-of-Pichincha-1822-+-Gran-Colombia-1822-1830 + Ecuador-security-crisis-2018-onwards-+-drug-cartel-violence-2024-distinctive + Noboa-presidency-2023 clusters within the corpus.
